title: Listen and Choose a Response — Campus Facilities

questions.stem: Excuse me, the printer on the second floor is out of paper again.

options.A: Thank you for letting me know. I'll refill it right away.
options.B: Printers often break down during finals.
options.C: You can print at the library instead.

questions.explanation: The speaker is reporting a facility problem, so acknowledging the report and offering to fix it is the appropriate staff response.

questions.stem: Am I allowed to eat in the study area?

options.A: Only covered drinks and small snacks are permitted.
options.C: I usually eat in the lounge.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about rules, so providing the specific policy directly answers their question.

questions.stem: Could you keep an eye on my bag while I grab a coffee? I'll be right back.

options.A: Sure, I'll watch it for you.
options.C: The cafe is on the first floor.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking a small favor, so a straightforward agreement is the expected response.

questions.stem: The new gym equipment is fantastic! I've been using it every day.

options.B: Exercise is important for your health.

questions.explanation: The speaker is sharing positive feedback, so acknowledging and validating their satisfaction is the natural response.

questions.stem: Don't forget to return your lab coat before leaving today.

options.A: Thanks for the reminder. I'll drop it off after class.
options.B: I bought my own lab coat.
options.C: The lab is closed on weekends.

questions.explanation: The speaker is giving a reminder, so acknowledging it and confirming compliance is the appropriate response.

questions.stem: I'm looking for a quiet place to study. Any suggestions?

options.A: The fourth floor of the library has individual study carrels.
options.B: Studying in groups is more effective.
options.C: I don't like studying at night.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for a recommendation, so providing a specific helpful suggestion directly addresses their need.

questions.stem: I can't make it to the seminar because I have a doctor's appointment.

options.A: I hope everything is okay. The slides will be posted online.
options.B: Doctors usually charge a lot for appointments.
options.C: Seminars are mandatory for all students.

questions.explanation: The speaker is explaining an absence, so expressing concern and offering a practical alternative is empathetic and helpful.

questions.stem: Is the tutoring center open on Sunday afternoons?
questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about hours of availability, so providing the specific schedule directly answers their question.

audioSegments.text: Excuse me, the printer on the second floor is out of paper again.
Am I allowed to eat in the study area?
Could you keep an eye on my bag while I grab a coffee? I'll be right back.
The new gym equipment is fantastic! I've been using it every day.
Don't forget to return your lab coat before leaving today.
I'm looking for a quiet place to study. Any suggestions?
I can't make it to the seminar because I have a doctor's appointment.
Is the tutoring center open on Sunday afternoons?
